entity: Golden State Mutual Life Insurance Company
explicit_text: Golden State Mutual was founded by Norman Houston, William Nickerson Jr., and myself to provide insurance services to the black community. We overcame legislative barriers and built the company by selling certificates of contribution and expanding to cities like Oakland and Chicago. Our philosophy was to create opportunities for young black employees and break racial barriers in the insurance industry.

His partnership was vital to our success in building a strong business that served the African American community.

In this role, I was deeply involved in public housing programs and faced political pressures, including controversies related to Chavez Ravine. My work aimed to improve housing conditions for the black community despite opposition.

Although I was active politically, I observed shifts in support from the Republican Party to the Democratic Party. Reagan's policies made it clear that progress required continued effort and vigilance.
